channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Button push on crossing times out and needs repressing to activate cross, 9th March
item.description:
Pedestrian crossing at Pirton corner
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cheltenham Road East, Churchdown, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Whole road of unsafe potholes, 9th March
item.description:
The entirety of the road outside the ladies college health and fitness centre. Children have tripped and hurt themselves because of the state of the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Malvern Road, Lansdown, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Collapsed trench across road, 8th March
item.description:
There is a collapsed trench just after the merge after the traffic lights heading away from GCHQ. Causing damage to suspensions
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Princess Elizabeth Way, Benhall, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big, quite deep pothole very close to right turn into The Reddings., 8th March
item.description:
Big, quite deep pothole very close to right turn into The Reddings coming from Churchdown direction. It's avoidable in daylight but could cause serious damage if hit. Difficult to miss in the dark. Very dangerous as close to frequently used turning. Has appeared in the last few days I think.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Badgeworth Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massive longitudinal potholes, 8th March
item.description:
On the road outside the Greyhound Pub and at the chicane near the playground. It is necessary to take evasive action to avoid these potholes and involves driving on the wrong side of the road, very dangerous situation

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Greyhound Gardens, Elmbridge, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waymarker removed from fence, path obscured, 8th March
item.description:
This issue was reported in August 2025 and there has been no action. The waymarker for this path has been removed, a sign directed into a different PRoW and a series of field gates now obscures the path. There has been no application to change, remove or divert this PRoW
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockhampton Lane, Kingsditch, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road surface material dumped in verge, 8th March
item.description:
Following recent repairs to some of the (many) potholes down this road, road surface material has been strewn in the verge by the contractor rather than being removed. This is disappointing and looks a mess, and is presumably not what is meant to happen to waste.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockhampton Lane, Kingsditch, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There is a sewage leak into the surface water drainage swale that runs beside the Coopers Edge Play Area. It is coming from the culvert than runs in from Glebe Hardens/ Guan Road. Upstream of the culvert the water is clear. The ducks that were there have gone. There is darks brown sludge and surface scum. Location on what three words around ///slap.anyone.splice but it continues towards the reed bed beside Lobleys Drive. It appeared 3 days ago and I have also reported it to the EA. Regards Claire Stewart, 8th March
item.description:
There is a sewage leak into the surface water drainage swale that runs beside the Coopers Edge Play Area. It is coming from the culvert than runs in from Glebe Hardens/ Guan Road. Upstream of the culvert the water is clear. The ducks that were there have gone. There is darks brown sludge and surface scum. Location on what three words around ///slap.anyone.splice but it continues towards the reed bed beside Lobleys Drive. It appeared 3 days ago and I have also reported it to the EA. Regards Claire Stewart

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carter Close, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Failed surface, 8th March
item.description:
The road surface has failed and is disintegrating entirely in this location forming potholes

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hawker Road, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road subsiding and numerous potholes, 8th March
item.description:
The road surface is subsiding and disintegrating right on a 90 degree bend in the road such that it is practically impossible to keep on the correct side of the road on a blind bend

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockhampton Lane, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Absolute disgrace, 8th March
item.description:
This is the damage to the road opposite Wade close in Hatherley lane Cheltenham. Apparently this is not a danger and they will keep an eye on it. The fact it's that who ever came to look at it thought that spraying a bit of white paint on it solved the problem, they need dismissal. This should be a priority repair, it stretches the width of a car, I have already damaged two wheels and have had to suffer a broken front spring. I imagine that those who came to look couldn't be bothered to repair it. If there is anymore damage to my car, white paint or not. I hope GCC responsible for any further damage.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hatherley Lane, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large deep pothole., 8th March
item.description:
Outside no 6 Woodmans Way in road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woodman's Way, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Edge of pavement, 8th March
item.description:
The pavement edge has been removed, making it uneven and dangerous, the kerb stone has been tampered with also. Not sure if 3 Fairhaven Avenue have done this to the pavement to get a huge caravan onto their property. I was walking my dog and the sudden jar to my hip that I've just had replaced was painful.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Fairhaven Avenue, Brockworth, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Farm equipment and electric fence blocking public right of way, 8th March
item.description:
Lots of farm equipment with an electric fence all around it is blocking the public right of way here. No signage and unkept path through the firld

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lorries / buses driving on grass verge since changes to double yellow lines, 8th March
item.description:
Grass verge outside 91 Gretton Road Since changes to double yellow lines cars have parked further up Gretton Road. Now lorries / buses coming up Gretton Road on the wrong side seem to be driving on grass verge to pass waiting queue of oncoming cars. The road is narrow here. Could the double yellow lines be extended to help avoid this?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gretton Road, Winchcombe, Winchcombe
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 large potholes outside my house on number 143 Hatherley Rd. This is incredibly unsafe every time a huge Bosco’s past my house and hits the pothole my entire house shakes. I am scared a car will lose its wheel or wheel trim resulting in the potential of my property / family being hit by flying objects. If a car loses control because it is hit one of these large potholes there is also the danger they will swerve off the road and potentially knock my family over who may be getting into their car via our drive. This is serious and needs sorting ASAP., 8th March
item.description:
Outside no 143 Hatherley Road… i’m concerned for the personal safety of my family when getting in our car on our open drive and of my property being hit through wheel trims coming off wheels, tyres blowing and cars swerving…. due to these multiple large hot holes.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 159, Hatherley Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple pot holes in succession, 8th March
item.description:
Outside gransmore gate
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sussex Gardens, Hucclecote, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole worsened since last review, 8th March
item.description:
Junction of Bournside Close and warden hill road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Bournside Close, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Part two additional potholes in the same location, 8th March
item.description:
Outside the old parsonage in Gretton just before the GWSR railway bridge

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gretton Road, Gretton, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Part one of a series of potholes, 8th March
item.description:
Series of potholes outside the Old Parsonage

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gretton Road, Gretton, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
